differential_diagnoses: Pyoderma gangrenosum associated with systemic neutrophilic disease (PASH syndrome)
Crohn's disease with metastatic (cutaneous) involvement and systemic complications
Systemic inflammatory response syndrome due to occult autoinflammatory disorder
next_steps: Perform deep wedge biopsy of an active ulcer edge for histopathology and tissue cultures (bacterial, fungal, mycobacterial)
Consult Dermatology and Rheumatology for urgent evaluation
Initiate high-dose intravenous glucocorticoids (e.g., methylprednisolone) as the primary management for presumed neutrophilic dermatosis
Perform colonoscopy to evaluate for subclinical Crohn's disease
Obtain skin/serum markers for autoinflammatory conditions (e.g., genetic testing for PSTPIP1 mutations)
Consider early transition to second-line immunosuppressive therapy (e.g., cyclosporine or TNF-inhibitors) if steroid-refractory
Discontinue broad-spectrum antibiotics once infectious etiology is ruled out by pathology and culture
